scenes.voiceover: You're embarrassed about your financial situation.
Maybe you have hidden credit card debt.
Low credit score.
No retirement savings.
You look around and feel like everyone else has it together.
Money shame makes you hide problems and avoid asking for help.
In America, talking about money is taboo.
So everyone pretends.
They post vacation photos while credit cards max out.
They drive nice cars they can't afford.
This silence keeps people stuck.
But most people are struggling with money somehow.
Consumer debt is at all-time highs.
Most Americans don't have $1,000 saved for emergencies.
You're not alone.
Money shame also prevents learning.
You're too embarrassed to admit you don't understand something.
You avoid financial advisors thinking they'll judge you.
The shame becomes a prison.
Breaking free starts with honesty.
First with yourself, then someone you trust.
Once you name the shame, it loses power.
But while shame is in control, you stay stuck.
